metadata.description: Required. The Azure region for all foundation resources.
Optional. Reverse the normal Cloud Adoption Framework naming convention by putting the resource type abbreviation at the end of the resource name.
Optional. Deploy the Secrets Key Vault (Standard SKU) for storing AVD credentials (VM admin password, domain join credentials).
Optional. Enable soft delete on the Secrets Key Vault.
Optional. Enable purge protection on the Secrets Key Vault. Recommended for production environments.
Optional. The retention period in days for soft-deleted objects in the Secrets Key Vault.
Optional. Virtual machine local administrator password. Stored as VirtualMachineAdminPassword in the Secrets Key Vault.
Optional. Virtual machine local administrator username. Stored as VirtualMachineAdminUserName in the Secrets Key Vault.
Optional. Domain join user password. Stored as DomainJoinUserPassword in the Secrets Key Vault.
Optional. Domain join user principal name (UPN). Stored as DomainJoinUserPrincipalName in the Secrets Key Vault.
Optional. Deploy the Encryption Key Vault (Premium SKU) for Customer-Managed Keys. Required when using CMK in any AVD solution.
Optional. Soft delete retention days for the Encryption Key Vault.

Optional. When true, the Encryption Key Vault is deployed with public network access enabled. Required when using CMK on a Recovery Services Vault with private endpoints, because Azure Backup does not use the AzureServices trusted service bypass.
Optional. Deploy private endpoints for the Key Vaults. When true, public network access is disabled on both Key Vaults.
Conditional. The resource ID of the subnet for Key Vault private endpoints. Required when privateEndpoint is true.
Conditional. The resource ID of the Azure Key Vault Private DNS Zone. Required when privateEndpoint is true.

Optional. Array of permitted IP addresses or CIDR blocks allowed through the firewall of all Key Vaults deployed by this module. Use when managing from a trusted workstation outside the Azure network boundary.
Optional. The resource ID of an existing Log Analytics Workspace for Key Vault diagnostic logs.
Optional. Tags to apply to deployed resources, keyed by resource type (e.g., "Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults", "Microsoft.Resources/resourceGroups").
DO NOT MODIFY THIS VALUE! The timestamp is needed to differentiate deployments for certain Azure resources and must be set using a parameter.
Optional. Soft delete retention days specifically for the Encryption Key Vault. Defaults to keyVaultRetentionInDays.
Optional. Array of permitted IP addresses or CIDR blocks allowed through the firewall of all Key Vaults deployed by this module.

Optional. When true, the encryption key vault is deployed with public network access enabled and all IP-based firewall restrictions cleared, regardless of the privateEndpoint setting. Required when CMK is configured on Recovery Services Vault (RSV does not use the AzureServices trusted service bypass, and its backup IPs are regional/dynamic so IP restrictions are not feasible). Only set this when you explicitly accept the tradeoff of an internet-reachable encryption key vault without IP restrictions.

variables.secretList: [union(if(not(empty(parameters('domainJoinUserPassword'))), createArray(createObject('name', 'DomainJoinUserPassword', 'value', parameters('domainJoinUserPassword'))), createArray()), if(not(empty(parameters('domainJoinUserPrincipalName'))), createArray(createObject('name', 'DomainJoinUserPrincipalName', 'value', parameters('domainJoinUserPrincipalName'))), createArray()), if(not(empty(parameters('virtualMachineAdminPassword'))), createArray(createObject('name', 'VirtualMachineAdminPassword', 'value', parameters('virtualMachineAdminPassword'))), createArray()), if(not(empty(parameters('virtualMachineAdminUserName'))), createArray(createObject('name', 'VirtualMachineAdminUserName', 'value', parameters('virtualMachineAdminUserName'))), createArray()))]

metadata.description: Category group name, e.g. "allLogs". Use either categoryGroup or category, not both.
Individual log category name. Use either categoryGroup or category, not both.
Optional. Override name for the diagnostic setting resource. If omitted, a deterministic name is generated from the workspace ID.
Resource ID of the Log Analytics workspace to send logs to.
Resource ID of the storage account to archive logs to.
Resource ID of the Event Hub authorization rule.
Name of the Event Hub. If omitted, a hub is created for each log category.
Log categories to stream. Defaults to allLogs when omitted.
Standard supports software-protected keys. Premium adds HSM-backed key support.
Allow ARM to retrieve secrets during deployments. Required if using KV secrets in parameter files.
Allow disk encryption operations (Azure Disk Encryption / Disk Encryption Sets) to access the vault.
Optional. Whether this vault is accessed via a private endpoint. When true and no permittedIPs are provided, public network access is disabled.
Optional. Array of permitted IP addresses or CIDR blocks. When provided, a deny-by-default firewall is applied.
Resource ID of the subnet to deploy the private endpoint into.
Resource ID of the resource to create the private endpoint for.
Group ID (sub-resource) of the private link service.
Optional. Custom name for the network interface created for this endpoint.
Optional. Private DNS zone group: resource IDs of private DNS zones to register this endpoint in.
Optional. Content type of the secret (e.g. "text/plain").
Category group name, e.g. "allLogs". Use either categoryGroup or category, not both.
Individual log category name. Use either categoryGroup or category, not both.
Optional. Override name for the diagnostic setting resource. If omitted, a deterministic name is generated from the workspace ID.
Resource ID of the Log Analytics workspace to send logs to.
Resource ID of the storage account to archive logs to.
Resource ID of the Event Hub authorization rule.
Name of the Event Hub. If omitted, a hub is created for each log category.
Log categories to stream. Defaults to allLogs when omitted.
Standard supports software-protected keys. Premium adds HSM-backed key support.
Allow ARM to retrieve secrets during deployments. Required if using KV secrets in parameter files.
Allow disk encryption operations (Azure Disk Encryption / Disk Encryption Sets) to access the vault.
Optional. Whether this vault is accessed via a private endpoint. When true and no permittedIPs are provided, public network access is disabled.
Optional. Array of permitted IP addresses or CIDR blocks. When provided, a deny-by-default firewall is applied.
Resource ID of the subnet to deploy the private endpoint into.
Resource ID of the resource to create the private endpoint for.
Group ID (sub-resource) of the private link service.
Optional. Custom name for the network interface created for this endpoint.
Optional. Private DNS zone group: resource IDs of private DNS zones to register this endpoint in.
The name of the security resource group.
The name of the Secrets Key Vault. Empty if not deployed.
The resource ID of the Secrets Key Vault. Pass as "credentialsKeyVaultResourceId" to the host pool and Session Host Replacer deployments.
The name of the Encryption Key Vault. Empty if not deployed.
The resource ID of the Encryption Key Vault. Pass as "encryptionKeyVaultResourceId" to any AVD solution using Customer-Managed Keys.
The URI of the Encryption Key Vault.
